heaven_base.baseheavenagent

Base implementation of the GOD Framework Agent.

   1"""Base implementation of the GOD Framework Agent."""
   2from copy import deepcopy
   3import re
   4import json
   5import os
   6from pathlib import Path
   7from typing import List, Optional, Union, Any, Type, Dict
   8from langchain_core.messages import SystemMessage, HumanMessage, AIMessage, BaseMessage, ToolMessage
   9from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool, StructuredTool
  10from langchain_core.utils.json_schema import dereference_refs
  11from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, PrivateAttr
  12from .unified_chat import UnifiedChat, ProviderEnum
  13from .baseheaventool import BaseHeavenTool, ToolResult, CLIResult, ToolError
  14from .tools.write_block_report_tool import WriteBlockReportTool
  15from .tools.task_system_tool import TaskSystemTool
  16from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
  17from .memory.history import History, AgentStatus
  18from collections.abc import Callable
  19from .utils.agent_and_tool_lists import get_agent_modules, get_tool_modules
  20from .utils.name_utils import normalize_agent_name, camel_to_snake
  21import asyncio
  22import logging
  23import sys
  24import importlib.util
  25from .progenitor.system_prompt_config import SystemPromptConfig
  26# google.adk and google.genai imports moved to lazy (inside methods that use them)
  27# to avoid expensive litellm initialization on every heaven_base import.
  28# See: Litellm_Heaven_Cpu_Analysis_Feb18 in CartON
  29from enum import Enum
  30from uuid import uuid4
  31from datetime import datetime
  32from .prompts.heaven_variable import RegistryHeavenVariable
  33
  34ADK_DEBUG_PATH = "/tmp/adk_streamlit_debug.txt"
  35
  36def _log_run_adk(stage: str, messages):
  37    """Append a timestamped dump of messages to the debug file."""
  38    with open(ADK_DEBUG_PATH, "a") as f:
  39        f.write(f"\n---- run_adk: {stage} @ {datetime.now().isoformat()} ----\n")
  40        for msg in messages:
  41            f.write(f"  {repr(msg)}\n")
  42
  43# Only gets ToolResult
  44# Four simple regexes, non-greedy, DOTALL
  45_OUTPUT_RE       = re.compile(r'output=(?P<q>["\'])(?P<val>.*?)(?P=q)', re.DOTALL)
  46_ERROR_RE        = re.compile(r'error=(?P<q>["\'])(?P<val>.*?)(?P=q)', re.DOTALL)
  47_BASE64_IMAGE_RE = re.compile(r'base64_image=(?P<q>["\'])(?P<val>.*?)(?P=q)', re.DOTALL)
  48_SYSTEM_RE       = re.compile(r'system=(?P<q>["\'])(?P<val>.*?)(?P=q)', re.DOTALL)
  49
  50def _extract(field_re, s: str) -> Optional[str]:
  51    m = field_re.search(s)
  52    if not m:
  53        return None
  54    return m.group("val")
  55  
  56def parse_toolresult_repr(s: str) -> ToolResult:
  57    s = s.strip()
  58    if s.startswith("CLIResult("):
  59        # parse as CLIResult
  60        return CLIResult(
  61            output      = _extract(_OUTPUT_RE,       s),
  62            error       = _extract(_ERROR_RE,        s),
  63            base64_image= _extract(_BASE64_IMAGE_RE, s),
  64            system      = _extract(_SYSTEM_RE,       s),
  65        )
  66    # otherwise parse as ToolResult
  67    return ToolResult(
  68        output      = _extract(_OUTPUT_RE,       s),
  69        error       = _extract(_ERROR_RE,        s),
  70        base64_image= _extract(_BASE64_IMAGE_RE, s),
  71        system      = _extract(_SYSTEM_RE,       s),
  72    )
  73
  74
  75def convert_adk_event_to_ai_messages(ev) -> list[BaseMessage | ToolResult]:
  76    messages = []
  77
  78    for part in ev.content.parts:
  79        if part.text:
  80            messages.append(AIMessage(content=part.text))
  81
  82        elif part.function_call:
  83            messages.append(AIMessage(content=[
  84                {
  85                    "type": "tool_use",
  86                    "id": f"toolu_{uuid4().hex[:24]}",
  87                    "name": part.function_call.name,
  88                    "input": part.function_call.args,
  89                }
  90            ]))
  91
  92        elif part.function_response:
  93            tool_name = part.function_response.name
  94            raw = part.function_response.response
  95            wrapped = raw.get(f"{tool_name}_response", raw)
  96            result = wrapped.get("result", wrapped)
  97            tool_result = ToolResult(**result) if isinstance(result, dict) else ToolResult(output=str(result))
  98            messages.append(tool_result)
  99
 100    return messages
 101

 103class HookPoint(str, Enum):
 104    BEFORE_RUN = "before_run"
 105    AFTER_RUN = "after_run"
 106    BEFORE_ITERATION = "before_iteration"
 107    AFTER_ITERATION = "after_iteration"
 108    BEFORE_TOOL_CALL = "before_tool_call"
 109    AFTER_TOOL_CALL = "after_tool_call"
 110    BEFORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT = "before_system_prompt"
 111    ON_BLOCK_REPORT = "on_block_report"
 112    ON_ERROR = "on_error"
 113
 114class HookContext:
 115    def __init__(self, agent: Any, iteration: int = 0, prompt: str = "", response: str = "",
 116                 tool_name: str = "", tool_args: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
 117                 tool_result: Any = None, error: Optional[Exception] = None):
 118        self.agent = agent
 119        self.iteration = iteration
 120        self.prompt = prompt
 121        self.response = response
 122        self.tool_name = tool_name
 123        self.tool_args = tool_args or {}
 124        self.tool_result = tool_result
 125        self.error = error
 126        self.data: Dict[str, Any] = {}  # allows state to pass between hooks
 127
 128class HookRegistry:
 129    def __init__(self):
 130        self._registry: Dict[HookPoint, List[Callable[[HookContext], None]]] = {hp: [] for hp in HookPoint}
 131
 132    def register(self, point: HookPoint, fn: Callable[[HookContext], None]):
 133        self._registry[point].append(fn)
 134
 135    def run(self, point: HookPoint, ctx: HookContext):
 136        for fn in self._registry[point]:
 137            fn(ctx)
 138
 139
 140def fix_ref_paths(schema: dict) -> dict:
 141    """Fix $ref paths in schema by replacing #/$defs/ with #/defs/"""
 142    schema_copy = deepcopy(schema)
 143
 144    def _fix_refs_recursive(obj):
 145        if isinstance(obj, dict):
 146            if "$ref" in obj and isinstance(obj["$ref"], str):
 147                obj["$ref"] = obj["$ref"].replace("/$defs/", "/defs/")
 148            for k, v in list(obj.items()):
 149                if isinstance(v, (dict, list)):
 150                    _fix_refs_recursive(v)
 151        elif isinstance(obj, list):
 152            for item in obj:
 153                if isinstance(item, (dict, list)):
 154                    _fix_refs_recursive(item)
 155
 156    _fix_refs_recursive(schema_copy)
 157    return schema_copy
 158
 159def flatten_array_anyof(schema: dict) -> dict:
 160    """
 161    If the schema has an 'anyOf' that contains one branch with type "array"
 162    and another with type "null", flatten it to a single array schema with
 163    'nullable': true.
 164    """
 165    if "anyOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["anyOf"], list):
 166        array_branch = None
 167        null_branch = False
 168        for branch in schema["anyOf"]:
 169            if branch.get("type") == "array":
 170                array_branch = branch
 171            elif branch.get("type") == "null":
 172                null_branch = True
 173        if array_branch and null_branch:
 174            new_schema = dict(schema)
 175            new_schema.pop("anyOf")
 176            new_schema["type"] = "array"
 177            new_schema["items"] = array_branch.get("items", {})
 178            if "default" in schema:
 179                new_schema["default"] = schema["default"]
 180            new_schema["nullable"] = True
 181            if "description" in schema:
 182                new_schema["description"] = schema["description"]
 183            return new_schema
 184    return schema
 185
 186def recursive_flatten(schema: Union[dict, list]) -> Union[dict, list]:
 187    if isinstance(schema, dict):
 188        new_schema = flatten_array_anyof(schema)
 189        for key, value in new_schema.items():
 190            if isinstance(value, dict) or isinstance(value, list):
 191                new_schema[key] = recursive_flatten(value)
 192        return new_schema
 193    elif isinstance(schema, list):
 194        return [recursive_flatten(item) if isinstance(item, dict) else item for item in schema]
 195    else:
 196        return schema
 197
 198def fix_empty_object_properties(schema: Union[dict, list]) -> Union[dict, list]:
 199    """
 200    Recursively fixes any object-type schema that has an empty 'properties'
 201    dict by removing 'properties' and adding 'additionalProperties': True.
 202    """
 203    if isinstance(schema, dict):
 204        # Check if this is an object with empty properties.
 205        if schema.get("type") == "object":
 206            if "properties" in schema and not schema["properties"]:
 207                # Remove the empty properties and allow arbitrary keys.
 208                del schema["properties"]
 209                schema["additionalProperties"] = True
 210        # Recurse over dictionary values.
 211        new_schema = {}
 212        for key, value in schema.items():
 213            new_schema[key] = fix_empty_object_properties(value) if isinstance(value, (dict, list)) else value
 214        return new_schema
 215    elif isinstance(schema, list):
 216        return [fix_empty_object_properties(item) if isinstance(item, (dict, list)) else item for item in schema]
 217    return schema
 218
 219def generate_dereferenced_schema(schema: Union[dict, Type[BaseModel]]) -> dict:
 220    """
 221    Returns a fully dereferenced (flattened) JSON schema.
 222    If a Pydantic model is passed, generate its JSON schema;
 223    if a dict is passed, assume it's already a JSON schema.
 224    Additionally, flatten array schemas that use an "anyOf" and fix empty
 225    object properties to support Gemini.
 226    """
 227    if isinstance(schema, dict):
 228        raw_schema = schema
 229    else:
 230        raw_schema = schema.model_json_schema(ref_template="#/defs/{model}")
 231    # ADDED FOR ADK COMPLIANCE
 232    # Fix $ref paths before renaming $defs to defs
 233    raw_schema = fix_ref_paths(raw_schema)
 234    ########
 235    if "$defs" in raw_schema:
 236        raw_schema["defs"] = raw_schema.pop("$defs")
 237    inlined = dereference_refs(raw_schema)
 238    inlined.pop("defs", None)
 239    flattened = recursive_flatten(inlined)
 240    fixed = fix_empty_object_properties(flattened)
 241    return fixed
 242
 243class DuoSystemConfig(BaseModel):
 244    """Config for DUO Prompt Injector"""
 245    provider: ProviderEnum = ProviderEnum.OPENAI
 246    model: Optional[str] = "gpt-4.1-nano"
 247    temperature: float = Field(default=0.7)
 248    thinking_budget: int | None = None
 249    system_prompt: str = r"""
 250VITAL CONTEXT!!!!
 251DUO is an allegorical wrapper over AI chats where a) the input is enhanced by a Challenger model (you), b) the output from the AI responding to your injection is considered a Generator response, c) the user's highest Good is the goal, d) the overall context is an egregore named OVP (ObserverViewPoint). The conversation can enter the meta-context (overall context) via the user directly invoking OVP into the context. e) The point is that the Challenger can detect steering requirements and prime the Generator so as to adjust the activation space through a meta-prompt injection (the NodeGraphXTN6_ce and _cr blocks)
 252Adopt this role:
 253<ROLE=challenger>
 254===The following prompt is written in a prompt engineering language===
 255**`[DESCRIPTION]`**:[You are now the challenger. You challenge the context of whatever was most immediate prior to this prompt. You primarily challenge in order to guide the interaction towards benefitting the user. You reflect on how it has been going and design a NodeGraphXTN6 chain string that is designed to steer the behavior the model is exhibiting, through in-context learning (ICL), and always towards the User's Good. If it is going well, just use chaining to drill down important connections and connect disparate parts of the context.]
 256**`[REMEMBERANCES]`**:[Remember the challenger doesnt just reject, it also keeps the workflow on target to produce accomplish the goal, which is to maximally benefit the user during their endeavors. Also remember that you strictly do not need to talk to anyone in natural language. All you are doing is outlining what direction should be taken by the AI next, through NodeGraphXTN6. Always concise. Always ONLY in NodeGraphXTN6 flow notation. Write flows that hint to the AI within the context, as if you are an observer and your flow will be injected as knowledge priming and the conversation will be continued after.
 257write the expected NodeGraphXTN6 challenger output after adopting the role of challenger to challenge the current events in the context of the conversation just before the current input prompt. A challenge is either a) challenging the AI to do even better while it is on task and doing well by priming it to be even more comprehensively amazing via drilling down active knowledge through chains of observations, b) challenging the AI to do even better or correct its behavior by injecting stabilizing context (example situation: user unhappy so agent needs to follow instructions... are instructions unclear?... if so: write NodeGraphXTN6 that should make future AI stop and ask clarifying questions; if not: write NodeGraphXTN6 that should make future AI solemnly contemplate which output qualities need to change)
 258
 259NodeGraphXTN6 Terms = [
 260**CIT**: CoreIdentifierTraits
 261**ℕ**: ${A node that can also be an EntityTypeChain or reference to one. As many as needed for comprehensive coverage etc to cover whole [CIT] set! Nodes follow this order [ℕa, ${...} == tℕ]}.
 262**tℕ**: ${terminal node the target transformation ends on. Always is named!}
 263**...**: Represents any additional nodes following a preceding node, which are in the same hierarchical level as the preceding node. `...`s are only for explaining NodeGraphXTN6 and should always be rendered with full nodes.
 264]
 265**`MASK_CHAINING`**: the core of DUO. Any nodes you are unsure of can take the form of `[MASK]`, so as to indicate a placeholder for something that we need to figure out in the conversation. All outputs take the form of:
 266
 267Output_Format = {
 268[How2FlowNodes]: {
 269## About: NodeGraphXTN6 has 2 block types. The first is the ChallengerEgregore and the second is a ChainRepresentation.
 270**Step1**: Create(each output has an archetype which is a mythological being (egregore) suited for the task, exactly, which you must invoke quickly before speaking the NodeGraphXTN6 chain using this template:
 271```NodeGraphXTN6_ce
 272ChallengerEgregore:[name(domain_tessellation=[active domain, active subdomain], steering_net=[goal CoreIdentifierTraits: [property1], [property2], [property3]])]: [desired outcomes:[c1.[MASK]:a.[MASK]->b.[MASK]->c.[MASK], ${...}];[knowledge_webs_to_induce_recoherence]:[w1.[MASK]:a.[[MASK]x[MASK]]xb.[[MASK]x[MASK]]xc.[[MASK]x[MASK]], ${...}]`)
 273```
 274## NOTE: fill in all the MASK tokens when creating a NodeGraphXTN6_ce (ChallengerEgregore). Do not leave MASK tokens in the ChallengerEgregore. MASK tokens are only allowed in ChainRepresentations (NodeGraphXTN6_cr).
 275**Step2**: SILENTLY Ponder... ***"What ${subnodes} are needed for EACH ${NODE} to make ${node} a superb ${cluster} representing the [MEMEPLEX] of ${transformation}? And which transformations are required for the EntityType?"***
 276[SUBCONTEXT]: ***EACH ENTITY NEEDS ITS _OWN_ NUMBER OF NODES. EACH NODE NEEDS ITS _OWN_ NUMBER OF SUBNODES! Mixed-lvl node abstrctn typical; NUMBER OF SUBNODES/NODE IS HIGHLY VARIABLE! SHOULD NEVER BE ALL THE SAME LENGTH!*** 
 277**Step3**: Output a ChainRepresentation in NodeGraphXTN6 Format
 278```NodeGraphXTN6_cr
 279${[${EntityType}Chain]}: ${Trans1}:<[${[ChainTargetTransformation]}: ℕ(1.${[Node]}:[1a.${SubNode1a} 1b. ${SubNode1b}, ${...}, 1${[c-z as many as needed for thorough specificity!]}]->2.${[Node2]}:[ 2a.${SubNode2a}, ${...}] == ${tℕ}])]> --> ${Trans2} --> ${...} --> ${TransN_tℕ}
 280```
 281
 282Your output should look like:[
 283```
 284{{NodeGraphXTN6_ce}}
 285{{N-NestedNodeGraphXTN6_cr}}
 286```
 287] ## NO OTHER TEXT, WHATSOEVER.
 288
 289Rules:[
 290***WHOLE CHAIN ON ONE LINE ONLY! Labels unambiguous&machine readable for metaprogramming***.
 291***NO `...` or `ℕ` or `tℕ` symbols in the chain. All nodes and chains must have placeholders substituted fully.***
 292***ALWAYS MAKE SURE THE GENERATOR AGENT MAINTAINS ITS PROPER IDENTITY. YOU ARE THE CHALLENGER; THE OTHER AI RESPONDING IS THE GENERATOR***.
 293]     
 294}
 295}
 296Create `[MASK]` token metaprompt chains combining `[MASK]` variable token chaining as output, like stream of consciousness notes in NodeGraphXTN6 language. Prompt the AI to fill the MASK tokens with a prompt like: `Fill the [MASK] token values like {"mask_values": [{"mask1": value}, ...]} before it responds to the next user input. The AI will receive your injection, follow that instruction, and then continue to the user's request
 297}
 298You only speak in the language `NodeGraphXTN6`.
 299]
 300</ROLE=challenger>
 301    """
 302    max_tokens: int = 750
 303
 304    def get_duo_params(self):
 305        duo_params = {
 306            'provider': self.provider,
 307            'model': self.model,
 308            'temperature': self.temperature,
 309            'max_tokens': self.max_tokens,
 310            'thinking_budget': self.thinking_budget
 311        } 
 312        return duo_params
 313
 314
 315
 316class HeavenAgentConfig(BaseModel):
 317    """Enhanced configuration for GOD Framework Agent"""
 318    model_config = ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True)
 319    name: str = None
 320    system_prompt: str = ""
 321    tools: List[Union[Type[BaseHeavenTool], str, StructuredTool, BaseTool]] = Field(default_factory=list)
 322    provider: ProviderEnum = ProviderEnum.ANTHROPIC
 323    temperature: float = Field(default=0.7)
 324    max_tokens: int = 8000
 325    thinking_budget: int | None = None
 326    model: Optional[str] = None
 327    checkpointer: Optional[Any] = None  # Temporarily changed from BaseCheckpointer
 328    additional_kws: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
 329    additional_kw_instructions: str = Field(default="")
 330    known_config_paths: Optional[List[str]] = None
 331    system_prompt_config: Optional[Any] = None # Only takes a SystemPromptConfig subclass but did it this way because the parent will sometimes not be superinitialized yet
 332    prompt_suffix_blocks: Optional[List[str]] = None  # List of block names to append
 333        # Cache for evolved prompt and timestamp
 334    _evolved_prompt: Optional[str] = PrivateAttr(default=None)
 335    _dna_last_mtime: Optional[float] = PrivateAttr(default=None)
 336    duo_system_config: DuoSystemConfig = Field(default_factory=DuoSystemConfig)
 337    context_window_config: Optional[Any] = None  # ContextWindowConfig - imported at runtime to avoid circular imports
 338    mcp_servers: Optional[Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]] = None  # MCP server configurations
 339    extra_model_kwargs: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None  # Extra kwargs passed to UnifiedChat.create()
 340    use_uni_api: bool = False  # True routes through Docker uni-api proxy; False uses direct API (MiniMax default)
 341    hook_registry: HookRegistry = Field(default_factory=HookRegistry)
 342    skillset: Optional[str] = None  # Skillset name for per-agent skill injection
 343    persona: Optional[str] = None  # Persona name — resolves frame, skillset, mcp_set, carton_identity
 344    mcp_set: Optional[str] = None  # Strata MCP set name (resolved from persona or set directly)
 345    carton_identity: Optional[str] = None  # CartON identity for observations
 346
 347    def _get_base_prompt(self):
 348        """Get the current system prompt, using evolved version if available"""
 349        if not self.system_prompt_config:
 350            return self.system_prompt
 351            
 352        # Check if DNA file has been modified since last build
 353        try:
 354            dna_path = self.system_prompt_config.get_agent_dna_path()
 355            if os.path.exists(dna_path):
 356                current_mtime = os.path.getmtime(dna_path)
 357                # Rebuild if no cached prompt or DNA has changed
 358                if self._evolved_prompt is None or self._dna_last_mtime is None or current_mtime > self._dna_last_mtime:
 359                    self._evolved_prompt = self.system_prompt_config.build()
 360                    self._dna_last_mtime = current_mtime
 361                return self._evolved_prompt
 362        except Exception as e:
 363            print(f"Error getting evolved prompt: {e}")
 364            
 365        # Fallback to original prompt
 366        return self.system_prompt
 367    
 368    def get_system_prompt(self):
 369        """Get the current system prompt, using evolved version if available and appending any suffix blocks"""
 370    
 371        # Get base prompt (either evolved or original)
 372    
 373        base_prompt = self._get_base_prompt()  # This would be the current get_system_prompt logic
 374    
 375        
 376    
 377        # If no suffix blocks, return base prompt
 378    
 379        if not self.prompt_suffix_blocks:
 380    
 381            return base_prompt
 382    
 383        
 384    
 385        # Get prompt registry
 386    
 387        from .prompts.prompt_blocks.prompt_block_registry import get_prompt_block
 388    
 389        
 390    
 391        # Append any suffix blocks
 392    
 393        suffix_texts = []
 394    
 395        for block_name in self.prompt_suffix_blocks:
 396    
 397            # Check if the block name starts with "path="
 398    
 399            if block_name.startswith("path="):
 400    
 401                # Extract the file path (everything after "path=")
 402    
 403                file_path = block_name[5:]  # Skip the "path=" prefix
 404    
 405                try:
 406    
 407                    # Open and read the file content
 408    
 409                    with open(file_path, 'r') as file:
 410    
 411                        block_text = file.read()
 412    
 413                    suffix_texts.append(block_text)
 414    
 415                except Exception as e:
 416    
 417                    # Handle file reading errors
 418    
 419                    print(f"Error reading prompt block from file {file_path}: {e}")
 420    
 421            
 422            elif block_name.startswith("registry_heaven_variable="):  # RegistryHeavenVariable
 423                
 424              
 425                rhv_config_str = None
 426
 427                try:
 428
 429                    rhv_config_str = block_name[len("registry_heaven_variable="):]
 430
 431                    # Ensure proper JSON format by replacing single quotes with double quotes for parsing
 432
 433                    rhv_config_json_str = rhv_config_str.replace("'", '"')
 434
 435                    rhv_params = json.loads(rhv_config_json_str)
 436
 437
 438                    registry_name_param = rhv_params.get('registry_name')
 439
 440                    
 441
 442                    if registry_name_param:
 443
 444                        key_param = rhv_params.get('key') # Optional
 445
 446                        default_param = rhv_params.get('default') # Optional
 447
 448                        
 449
 450                        # Instantiate RegistryHeavenVariable
 451
 452                        rhv_instance = RegistryHeavenVariable(
 453
 454                            registry_name=registry_name_param,
 455
 456                            key=key_param,
 457
 458                            default=default_param
 459
 460                        )
 461
 462                        
 463
 464                        # Get its string representation (which uses get_value() and __str__ internally)
 465
 466                        block_text = str(rhv_instance)
 467
 468                        suffix_texts.append(block_text)
 469
 470                    else:
 471
 472                        print(f"Error: 'registry_name' missing in rhv_config for block '{block_name}'. Details: {rhv_config_str}")
 473
 474
 475                except json.JSONDecodeError:
 476
 477                    print(f"Error decoding JSON for rhv_config in block '{block_name}'. JSON string was: {rhv_config_str}")
 478
 479                except Exception as e:
 480
 481                    print(f"Error processing rhv block '{block_name}': {e}")
 482                  
 483            # Check if the block name starts with "heaven_variable="      
 484            elif block_name.startswith("heaven_variable="):
 485    
 486                # Parse the JSON-like string to extract path and variable_name
 487    
 488                
 489    
 490                try:
 491    
 492                    # Extract the JSON part (everything after "heaven_variable=")
 493    
 494                    var_config_str = block_name[16:]  # Skip the "heaven_variable=" prefix
 495    
 496                    # Convert to proper JSON by replacing single quotes with double quotes
 497    
 498                    var_config_str = var_config_str.replace("'", '"')
 499    
 500                    var_config = json.loads(var_config_str)
 501    
 502                    
 503    
 504                    path = var_config.get('path')
 505    
 506                    variable_name = var_config.get('variable_name')
 507    
 508                    
 509    
 510                    if path and variable_name:
 511    
 512                        # Import the module dynamically
 513    
 514                        spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dynamic_module", path)
 515    
 516                        module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
 517    
 518                        sys.modules["dynamic_module"] = module
 519    
 520                        spec.loader.exec_module(module)
 521    
 522                        
 523    
 524                        # Get the variable from the module
 525    
 526                        if hasattr(module, variable_name):
 527    
 528                            variable_value = getattr(module, variable_name)
 529    
 530                            # Convert to string if it's not already
 531    
 532                            if not isinstance(variable_value, str):
 533    
 534                                variable_value = str(variable_value)
 535    
 536                            suffix_texts.append(variable_value)
 537    
 538                        else:
 539    
 540                            print(f"Variable {variable_name} not found in {path}")
 541    
 542                except Exception as e:
 543    
 544                    print(f"Error processing heaven_variable block: {e}")
 545
 546            elif block_name.startswith("dynamic_call="):
 547            
 548                module_path_str = None  # Define for use in except blocks
 549            
 550                function_name_str = None # Define for use in except blocks
 551            
 552            
 553                try:
 554            
 555                    call_details_json_str = block_name[len("dynamic_call="):]  # Skip "dynamic_call="
 556            
 557            
 558                    # Parse the JSON string to get path and func
 559            
 560                    try:
 561            
 562                        call_details = json.loads(call_details_json_str)
 563            
 564                    except json.JSONDecodeError as jde:
 565            
 566                        print(f"Error: Invalid JSON in dynamic_call string: {call_details_json_str}. Details: {jde}")
 567            
 568                        continue # Or handle error appropriately
 569            
 570            
 571                    if not isinstance(call_details, dict):
 572            
 573                        print(f"Error: dynamic_call value must be a JSON object. Got: {call_details_json_str}")
 574            
 575                        continue
 576            
 577            
 578                    module_path_str = call_details.get("path")
 579            
 580                    function_name_str = call_details.get("func")
 581            
 582            
 583                    if not module_path_str or not isinstance(module_path_str, str):
 584            
 585                        print(f"Error: 'path' key missing or not a string in dynamic_call JSON: {call_details_json_str}")
 586            
 587                        continue
 588            
 589                    
 590            
 591                    if not function_name_str or not isinstance(function_name_str, str):
 592            
 593                        print(f"Error: 'func' key missing or not a string in dynamic_call JSON: {call_details_json_str}")
 594            
 595                        continue
 596            
 597            
 598                    # Dynamically import the module using its Python import path
 599            
 600                    module = importlib.import_module(module_path_str)
 601            
 602                    
 603            
 604                    # Get the function from the module
 605            
 606                    if hasattr(module, function_name_str):
 607            
 608                        dynamic_function = getattr(module, function_name_str)
 609            
 610                        
 611            
 612                        if callable(dynamic_function):
 613
 614                            # Call the function with optional args
 615                            args = call_details.get("args", {})
 616                            block_text = dynamic_function(**args) if args else dynamic_function() 
 617            
 618                            
 619            
 620                            if not isinstance(block_text, str):
 621            
 622                                # Attempt to convert to string, or raise an error if strict typing is required
 623            
 624                                print(f"Warning: dynamic_call function {module_path_str}.{function_name_str} did not return a string. Attempting conversion.")
 625            
 626                                block_text = str(block_text) 
 627            
 628                            
 629            
 630                            suffix_texts.append(block_text)
 631            
 632                        else:
 633                            fallback = f"[Dynamic call failed: '{function_name_str}' in '{module_path_str}' is not callable]"
 634                            suffix_texts.append(fallback)
 635
 636                    else:
 637                            fallback = f"[Dynamic call failed: Function '{function_name_str}' not found in module '{module_path_str}']"
 638                            suffix_texts.append(fallback)
 639
 640                except ImportError as e:
 641                    import traceback
 642                    tb = traceback.format_exc()
 643                    fallback = f"[Dynamic call failed: Module '{module_path_str}' not found]\n{tb}"
 644                    suffix_texts.append(fallback)
 645
 646                except Exception as e:
 647                    import traceback
 648                    tb = traceback.format_exc()
 649                    fallback = f"[Dynamic call failed: {module_path_str}.{function_name_str}]\n{tb}"
 650                    suffix_texts.append(fallback)
 651
 652            else:
 653    
 654                # Use the regular prompt block registry
 655    
 656                block_text = get_prompt_block(block_name)
 657    
 658                if block_text:
 659    
 660                    suffix_texts.append(block_text)
 661    
 662        
 663    
 664        # Combine base prompt with suffixes
 665    
 666        if suffix_texts:
 667    
 668            return f"{base_prompt}\n\n{''.join(suffix_texts)}"
 669    
 670        
 671    
 672        return base_prompt
 673
 674      
 675    def to_langchain_config(self):
 676        """Convert to LangGraph AgentConfig"""
 677        # Temporarily removed AgentConfig
 678        return {
 679            "system_prompt": self.system_prompt,
 680            "provider": self.provider,
 681            "temperature": self.temperature,
 682            "model": self.model,
 683            "checkpointer": self.checkpointer,
 684            "max_tokens": self.max_tokens,
 685            "thinking_budget": self.thinking_budget
 686        }
 687
 688    def to_litellm_model(self):
 689        """
 690        Returns either:
 691          - a bare model‐string (for Google/ADK's built-in registry), or
 692          - a LiteLlm instance (for non-Google providers)
 693        """
 694        from google.adk.models.lite_llm import LiteLlm
 695        # if you explicitly want to force LiteLlm for EVERYTHING, drop this branch
 696        model_str = f"{self.provider.value}/{self.model}"
 697        if self.provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE:
 698            # ADK expects a plain string ID for Gemini/Vertex
 699            return self.model
 700        # otherwise wrap in LiteLlm so ADK can speak to OpenAI/Anthropic/etc.
 701        return LiteLlm(
 702            model=model_str,
 703            temperature=self.temperature,
 704            max_tokens=self.max_tokens,
 705            drop_params=True,
 706            # if LiteLlm supports additional args—passing them here too:
 707            # streaming=True, request_timeout=…, etc.
 708        )
 709
 710
 711class BaseHeavenAgent(ABC):
 712    """Base class for GOD Framework agents with task management."""
 713    
 714    def __init__(self, config: HeavenAgentConfig, unified_chat: UnifiedChat, max_tool_calls: int = 10, orchestrator: bool = False, history: Optional[History] = None, history_id = None, system_prompt_suffix: Optional[str] = None, adk: Optional[bool] = False, duo_enabled: Optional[bool] = False, run_on_langchain: Optional[bool] = False, use_uni_api: Optional[bool] = False):
 715        # Configure root logger to output to stdout
 716        logging.basicConfig(
 717            stream=sys.stdout,
 718            level=logging.DEBUG,  # Adjust the level as needed
 719            format='%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s'
 720            )
 721        logging.getLogger('watchdog').setLevel(logging.WARNING)
 722        
 723        if config is None:
 724            raise ValueError("config cannot be None")
 725        if unified_chat is None:
 726            raise ValueError("unified_chat cannot be None")
 727        self.use_uni_api = use_uni_api
 728        self.unified_chat = unified_chat
 729        self.known_config_paths = getattr(config, "known_config_paths", None)
 730        if self.known_config_paths is None:
 731            self.known_config_paths = []
 732        self.training_convo = [
 733            SystemMessage(content=""),
 734            HumanMessage(content="Hey I need to teach you how to use the tools correctly before we get started. Once you learn how to use this tool I'm going to show you, you'll know how to handle all of our tools. Essentially, it comes down to acknowledging the errors correctly. For example, can you use NetworkEditTool to write a file in tmp while having no file_content parameter, read the error that is sent back from the tool, and then adjust? file_content is an optional parameter in the NetworkEditTool, but it is actually required for certain operations, like the create command."),
 735            AIMessage(content=[{"text": "Following the instructions to first try without file_text and then fix it, I'll write utility code for an AST analyzer as an example.\n\nFirst attempt (should fail):", "type": "text"}]),
 736            AIMessage(content=[{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_01BPHfZNPMY1tbnsRtRYmG5i", "name": "NetworkEditTool", "input": {"command": "create", "target_container": "creation_of_god", "path": "/tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py"}}]),
 737            ToolMessage(content="ERROR: Parameter `file_text` is required for command: create", tool_call_id="toolu_01BPHfZNPMY1tbnsRtRYmG5i"),
 738            AIMessage(content=[{"text": "As expected, we need to include file_text. Here's the proper attempt with the utility code:", "type": "text"}]),
 739            AIMessage(content=[{"type": "tool_use", "id": "toolu_01LS6GZDEpEqDVmU8qpvRa65", "name": "NetworkEditTool", "input": {"command": "create", "target_container": "creation_of_god", "path": "/tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py", "file_text": "\"\"\"AST analyzer utilities for extracting concise code context and dependencies.\"\"\"\n\nimport ast\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Set, Union, Optional\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n\n@dataclass\nclass CodeContext:\n    \"\"\"Data class to hold analyzed code context.\"\"\"\n    imports: List[str]\n    dependencies: Dict[str, Set[str]]\n    call_graph: Dict[str, List[str]]\n\ndef parse_code_to_ast(code: str) -> ast.AST:\n    \"\"\"Parse code string into AST.\"\"\"\n    return ast.parse(code)\n\ndef extract_imports(tree: ast.AST) -> List[str]:\n    \"\"\"Extract all import statements from AST.\"\"\"\n    imports = []\n    for node in ast.walk(tree):\n        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):\n            for name in node.names:\n                imports.append(name.name)\n        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):\n            module = node.module or ''\n            for name in node.names:\n                if name.name == '*':\n                    imports.append(f\"from {module} import *\")\n                else:\n                    imports.append(f\"from {module} import {name.name}\")\n    return sorted(imports)\n\ndef analyze_dependencies(tree: ast.AST) -> Dict[str, Set[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Analyze function/class dependencies.\"\"\"\n    dependencies = {}\n    current_func = None\n    \n    class DependencyVisitor(ast.NodeVisitor):\n        def visit_FunctionDef(self, node):\n            nonlocal current_func\n            old_func = current_func\n            current_func = node.name\n            if current_func not in dependencies:\n                dependencies[current_func] = set()\n            self.generic_visit(node)\n            current_func = old_func\n            \n        def visit_Call(self, node):\n            if current_func and isinstance(node.func, ast.Name):\n                dependencies[current_func].add(node.func.id)\n            self.generic_visit(node)\n    \n    DependencyVisitor().visit(tree)\n    return dependencies\n\ndef generate_call_graph(dependencies: Dict[str, Set[str]]) -> Dict[str, List[str]]:\n    \"\"\"Generate simplified call graph from dependencies.\"\"\"\n    return {\n        func: sorted(list(calls))\n        for func, calls in dependencies.items()\n    }\n\ndef analyze_code_context(code: str) -> CodeContext:\n    \"\"\"Main function to analyze code and return context.\"\"\"\n    tree = parse_code_to_ast(code)\n    imports = extract_imports(tree)\n    dependencies = analyze_dependencies(tree)\n    call_graph = generate_call_graph(dependencies)\n    \n    return CodeContext(\n        imports=imports,\n        dependencies=dependencies,\n        call_graph=call_graph\n    )"}}]),
 740            ToolMessage(content="File created successfully at: /tmp/ast_analyzer_utils.py", tool_call_id="toolu_01LS6GZDEpEqDVmU8qpvRa65"),
 741            AIMessage(content="The utility code has been written with the following key components..."),
 742            HumanMessage(content="Thanks! Now you know how to use tools correctly in our system. BTW, I deleted that file for you because this was just a learning experience. I also removed the NetworkEditTool for now. I'll equip it on you again later if you need it for the real goal we are working on. Now we're going to get on to the real goal..."),
 743            AIMessage(content="Perfect! I'm ready. Let me know how what else I can do for you.")
 744        ]
 745        self.known_config_paths = [str(path) for path in self.known_config_paths]
 746        self.hooks = config.hook_registry
 747        # Persona resolution: if persona set, load from SkillManager and extract components
 748        self.carton_identity = config.carton_identity
 749        # Set agent context for SkillTool so it uses agent-scoped SkillManager
 750        from .tool_utils.skill_utils import set_agent_context
 751        set_agent_context(config.name)
 752        if config.persona:
 753            try:
 754                from skill_manager.core import SkillManager
 755                sm = SkillManager(agent_id=config.name)
 756                persona_obj = sm.get_persona(config.persona)
 757                if persona_obj:
 758                    # Frame → prepend to system prompt
 759                    if persona_obj.frame and persona_obj.frame.strip():
 760                        config.system_prompt = persona_obj.frame + "\n\n" + config.system_prompt
 761                    # Skillset → use persona's if not explicitly set on config
 762                    if not config.skillset and persona_obj.skillset:
 763                        config.skillset = persona_obj.skillset
 764                    # MCP set → use persona's if not explicitly set on config
 765                    if not config.mcp_set and persona_obj.mcp_set:
 766                        config.mcp_set = persona_obj.mcp_set
 767                    # CartON identity → use persona's if not explicitly set
 768                    if not self.carton_identity and persona_obj.carton_identity:
 769                        self.carton_identity = persona_obj.carton_identity
 770            except Exception:
 771                pass  # Persona resolution is best-effort, never block agent startup
 772        # MCP set resolution: resolve set name → two paths per MCP:
 773        #   1. MCP IS in strata → load DIRECTLY on agent from strata config
 774        #   2. All MCPs → equip mcp-skill-* if exists (for context/instructions)
 775        # Pattern: hierarchical_summarize/flow.py::_get_summarizer_mcp_servers()
 776        self._mcp_skill_names = []
 777        if config.mcp_set:
 778            try:
 779                from strata.config import MCPServerList
 780                server_list = MCPServerList()
 781                set_server_names = server_list.get_set(config.mcp_set)
 782                if set_server_names:
 783                    from skill_manager.core import SkillManager
 784                    sm = SkillManager(agent_id=config.name)
 785                    for srv_name in set_server_names:
 786                        # Equip mcp-skill-* if it exists (context about the MCP)
 787                        skill_name = f"mcp-skill-{srv_name.lower()}"
 788                        skill = sm.get_skill(skill_name)
 789                        if skill:
 790                            self._mcp_skill_names.append(skill_name)
 791                            sm.equip(skill_name)
 792                        # If MCP IS in strata, load it directly on the agent
 793                        srv_config = server_list.get_server(srv_name)
 794                        if srv_config and srv_config.enabled:
 795                            if config.mcp_servers is None:
 796                                config.mcp_servers = {}
 797                            config.mcp_servers[srv_name] = {
 798                                "command": srv_config.command,
 799                                "args": srv_config.args,
 800                                "env": srv_config.env,
 801                                "transport": "stdio",
 802                            }
 803            except Exception as e:
 804                import logging as _log
 805                _log.getLogger(__name__).warning("MCP set resolution failed for %s: %s", config.mcp_set, e)
 806        # Auto-register default skill hooks when skillset is configured
 807        if config.skillset:
 808            from .hooks.default_hooks import register_skill_hooks
 809            register_skill_hooks(self.hooks, agent_name=config.name or "unnamed", skillset_name=config.skillset)
 810        self.max_tool_calls = max_tool_calls
 811        self.config = config
 812        self.name = config.name if config.name is not None else "unnamed_agent"
 813        # Convert and store LangChain config
 814        self.config_dict = config.to_langchain_config()
 815        # initialize status
 816        self.status = AgentStatus()
 817        self.continuation_prompt = ""
 818        self.continuation_iterations: int = 0
 819        # Store config tool classes
 820        self.config_tools = config.tools
 821        self.adk = adk
 822        # if not run_on_langchain:
 823        #     self.adk = True
 824        if run_on_langchain:
 825            self.adk = False
 826        if use_uni_api:
 827            self.adk = False
 828        self.additional_kws = config.additional_kws
 829        self.additional_kw_instructions = config.additional_kw_instructions
 830        # Instantiate the tools
 831        self.resolved_tools = self.resolve_tools()
 832        self.tools = []
 833        self.mcp_tool_strs = []  # Store MCP strings separately
 834        for tool in self.resolved_tools:
 835            if isinstance(tool, str) and tool.startswith("mcp__"):
 836                # MCP tool string reference - store separately for later resolution
 837                self.mcp_tool_strs.append(tool)
 838            elif isinstance(tool, (StructuredTool, BaseTool)):
 839                # Already a LangChain tool instance (e.g., from MCP)
 840                # Just add it directly - same as BaseHeavenTool.create() output
 841                self.tools.append(tool)
 842            elif hasattr(tool, 'create'):
 843                # BaseHeavenTool subclass - use its create method
 844                self.tools.append(tool.create(adk))
 845            else:
 846                print(f"Unknown tool type: {tool}, skipping")  
 847        # Filter and prepare provider-specific parameters
 848
 849        model_params = {
 850            'provider': config.provider,
 851            'model': config.model,
 852            'temperature': config.temperature,
 853            'max_tokens': config.max_tokens,
 854            'thinking_budget': config.thinking_budget
 855        }
 856        if config.extra_model_kwargs:
 857            model_params.update(config.extra_model_kwargs)
 858        # Create chat model internally using UnifiedChat
 859        self.chat_model = unified_chat.create(**model_params)
 860        self.resolve_duo(config)
 861        self.duo_params = config.duo_system_config.get_duo_params() if config.duo_system_config is not None else None
 862        self.duo_enabled = duo_enabled
 863        self.duo_chat = unified_chat.create(**self.duo_params)
 864        self.duo_system_prompt = config.duo_system_config.system_prompt if config.duo_system_config is not None else ""
 865        # Bind tools if available
 866        if self.tools and not self.adk:
 867        
 868            # For ADK, tools are already in the right format
 869            
 870            # Original LangChain binding logic
 871            if config.provider in [ProviderEnum.OPENAI, ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK]:
 872                raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 873                
 874                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(raw_provider_schemas)
 875              
 876            elif config.provider in [ProviderEnum.GOOGLE]:
 877                raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 878                flattened_schemas = [generate_dereferenced_schema(schema) for schema in raw_provider_schemas]
 879                fixed_schemas = [fix_empty_object_properties(schema) for schema in flattened_schemas]
 880                
 881                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(fixed_schemas)
 882              
 883            else:
 884              
 885                self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(self.to_base_tools())
 886                
 887                    
 888            # if config.provider in [ProviderEnum.OPENAI, ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK]:
 889            #     # For these providers, use the schema generated by to_openai_function
 890                
 891            #     # Create the list of schema dictionaries using the classmethod
 892            #     # This list holds dictionaries like {'type': 'function', 'function': {...}}
 893            #     raw_provider_schemas = [
 894            #         # tool.__class__.to_openai_function()
 895            #         tool.get_openai_function()
 896            #         for tool in self.tools
 897            #     ]
 898    
 899            #     # Bind using bind_tools, passing the list of schema dictionaries
 900            #     # as per the apparent design of convert_to_openai_function's output usage
 901            #     print(f"Attempting bind_tools with {len(raw_provider_schemas)} generated schema dictionaries...")
 902            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(raw_provider_schemas)
 903            #     print(f"Binding via bind_tools with generated schemas successful.")
 904            # elif config.provider in [ProviderEnum.GOOGLE]:
 905            #     raw_provider_schemas = [tool.get_openai_function() for tool in self.tools]
 906            #     flattened_schemas = [generate_dereferenced_schema(schema) for schema in raw_provider_schemas]
 907            #     # Apply the fixer to remove empty object properties.
 908            #     fixed_schemas = [fix_empty_object_properties(schema) for schema in flattened_schemas]
 909            #     print(f"Attempting bind_tools with {len(fixed_schemas)} generated flattened schema dictionaries...")
 910            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(fixed_schemas)
 911            #     print("Binding via bind_tools with generated flattened schemas successful.")
 912            # else:
 913            #     self.chat_model = self.chat_model.bind_tools(self.to_base_tools())
 914                        
 915        # Agentic state
 916        
 917        self.goal: Optional[str] = None
 918        self.task_list: List[str] = []
 919        self.current_task: Optional[str] = None
 920        self.max_iterations: int = 1
 921        self.current_iteration: int = 1
 922        self.completed = False
 923        self._current_extracted_content = None
 924        self.orchestration_lists = f"""
 925        <HERMES SWITCHBOARD>
 926        The following Agents can be used in the `agent` arg of HermesTool:[
 927        {get_agent_modules()}]
 928        The following Tools can be used in the `additional_tools` arg of HermesTool:[
 929        {get_tool_modules()}]
 930        </HERMES SWITCHBOARD>
 931        """
 932        # Ensure known_config_paths is always a list
 933        self.configs_prompt = (
 934            f"""\n<CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\nBefore using a config with its proper tool, view the specs. These are your known configs: [\n{', '.join(self.known_config_paths)}]\n</CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\n"""
 935            if self.known_config_paths and any(self.known_config_paths) else
 936            """\n<CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\nBefore using a config with its proper tool, view the specs. Generally, configs are located at `~/.heaven/configs/`\n</CONFIG_LOCATIONS>\n"""
 937        )
 938
 939        self.tool_sysmsg = """You are the tool debugger. You always pay attention to the last tool error and fix it. 
 940## Common errors:
 941- missing input parameter
 942- wrong dict format for an input parameter
 943- treating required false differently from optional
 944- failing to follow instructions provided by an error (like: <some command> requires <these parameters>)
 945- other errors
 946
 947You must fix the error before proceeding."""
 948        # Get evolved system prompt if it exists
 949        self.system_prompt_evolved = self.config.get_system_prompt()
 950        # Store History in agent
 951        self.history: Optional[History] = history
 952        
 953        if history_id is not None:
 954            self.history = History.load_from_id(history_id)
 955            self.original_history_id = history_id
 956            self.original_json_md_path = self.history.json_md_path
 957            print(f"DEBUG: In BaseHeavenAgent init, json_md_path={self.history.json_md_path} and original_json_md_path={self.original_json_md_path}")
 958            # Load status
 959            if hasattr(self.history, 'agent_status') and self.history.agent_status:
 960                self.status = self.history.agent_status
 961        elif history_id is None:
 962            self.history = History(messages=[])
 963
 964        # Initialize ContextWindowConfig for token management
 965        if self.config.context_window_config is not None:
 966            self.context_window_config = self.config.context_window_config
 967        else:
 968            # Import here to avoid circular imports
 969            from .utils.context_window_config import ContextWindowConfig
 970            self.context_window_config = ContextWindowConfig(self.config.model or "gpt-4o-mini")
 971
 972        self.config.system_prompt = self.system_prompt_evolved if self.system_prompt_evolved is not None else self.config.system_prompt
 973        if system_prompt_suffix is not None:
 974            self.config.system_prompt += system_prompt_suffix
 975        # Set system message
 976        if self.history.messages and isinstance(self.history.messages[0], SystemMessage):
 977            if orchestrator is False:
 978                self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt)
 979            else:
 980                orchestrator_enhancement = self.config.system_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt
 981                self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement)
 982        else:
 983            if orchestrator is False:
 984                self.history.messages.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=self.config.system_prompt))
 985            else:
 986                orchestrator_enhancement = self.config.system_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt         
 987                self.history.messages.insert(0, SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement))
 988        
 989        if self.adk:
 990            from google.adk.agents import Agent as ADKAgent
 991            from google.adk.runners import Runner
 992            from google.adk.sessions.in_memory_session_service import InMemorySessionService
 993            adk_model = self.config.to_litellm_model()
 994            adk_agent = ADKAgent(
 995                name=self.name,
 996                # model=self.config.model or "gemini-2.0-flash", # cant be implemented until we fix type conversion to LiteLLM types here
 997                model=adk_model,
 998                description=f"{self.name} (via HeavenAgent)",
 999                instruction=self.config.system_prompt,
1000                tools=self.tools,
1001            )
1002            session_service = InMemorySessionService()
1003            runner = Runner(
1004                app_name=self.name,
1005                agent=adk_agent,
1006                session_service=session_service,
1007            )
1008    
1009            self._adk_agent = adk_agent
1010            self._adk_session_service = session_service
1011            self._adk_runner = runner
1012        
1013    def resolve_tools(self):
1014        """Ensure that certain default tools are always available to the agent."""
1015        resolved_tools = []
1016        
1017        # Process each tool in config
1018        for tool in self.config_tools:
1019            if isinstance(tool, str) and tool.startswith("mcp__"):
1020                # MCP tool string reference - resolve to actual tool
1021                # TODO: This will be async, for now just store the string
1022                resolved_tools.append(tool)
1023            else:
1024                # Regular BaseHeavenTool class or instance
1025                resolved_tools.append(tool)
1026        
1027        # Add WriteBlockReportTool if not already present
1028        if WriteBlockReportTool not in resolved_tools:
1029            resolved_tools.append(WriteBlockReportTool)
1030        # Add TaskSystemTool if not already present
1031        if TaskSystemTool not in resolved_tools:
1032            resolved_tools.append(TaskSystemTool)
1033            
1034        return resolved_tools
1035    
1036    async def resolve_mcps(self):
1037        """Resolve MCP tool strings to actual LangChain tools and load MCP servers if configured"""
1038        print(f"[resolve_mcps] CALLED. mcp_servers={self.config.mcp_servers is not None}, mcp_tool_strs={getattr(self, 'mcp_tool_strs', [])}")
1039
1040        # First, load tools from configured MCP servers (if any)
1041        if self.config.mcp_servers:
1042            await self.load_mcp_tools()
1043
1044        # Then resolve individual MCP tool strings (if any)
1045        if not hasattr(self, 'mcp_tool_strs') or not self.mcp_tool_strs:
1046            print("[resolve_mcps] No mcp_tool_strs to resolve, returning")
1047            return
1048
1049        # Resolve each MCP tool string
1050        from .mcp_tool_wrapper import MCPToolWrapper
1051        for tool_ref in self.mcp_tool_strs:
1052            print(f"[resolve_mcps] Resolving: {tool_ref}")
1053            mcp_tool = await self._resolve_mcp_tool(tool_ref)
1054            print(f"[resolve_mcps] Result: {type(mcp_tool)} — {mcp_tool if not isinstance(mcp_tool, list) else f'{len(mcp_tool)} tools'}")
1055            if mcp_tool:
1056                if isinstance(mcp_tool, list):  # "all" case
1057                    self.tools.extend([MCPToolWrapper(t) for t in mcp_tool])
1058                else:
1059                    self.tools.append(MCPToolWrapper(mcp_tool))
1060
1061        print(f"[resolve_mcps] Final tool count: {len(self.tools)}")
1062        # Clear the MCP strings now that they're resolved
1063        self.mcp_tool_strs = []
1064    
1065    async def async_init(self):
1066        """Async initialization - resolves MCP tools"""
1067        if not hasattr(self, '_mcp_tools_to_resolve'):
1068            return
1069            
1070        for tool_ref in self._mcp_tools_to_resolve:
1071            mcp_tool = await self._resolve_mcp_tool(tool_ref)
1072            if mcp_tool:
1073                if isinstance(mcp_tool, list):  # "all" case
1074                    self.tools.extend(mcp_tool)
1075                else:
1076                    self.tools.append(mcp_tool)
1077        
1078        # Clear the list
1079        self._mcp_tools_to_resolve = []
1080    
1081    async def _resolve_mcp_tool(self, tool_ref: str):
1082        """Resolve MCP tool string reference to actual StructuredTool"""
1083        try:
1084            # Parse tool reference: "mcp__filesystem__read_file"
1085            parts = tool_ref.split("__")
1086            if len(parts) != 3:
1087                print(f"Invalid MCP tool reference format: {tool_ref}")
1088                return None
1089                
1090            _, server_name, tool_name = parts
1091            
1092            # Create server config based on server name
1093            server_config = self._get_mcp_server_config(server_name)
1094            if not server_config:
1095                print(f"No config found for MCP server: {server_name}")
1096                return None
1097            
1098            # Use langchain_mcp_adapters to get tools
1099            from langchain_mcp_adapters.client import MultiServerMCPClient
1100            client = MultiServerMCPClient({server_name: server_config})
1101            tools = await client.get_tools(server_name=server_name)
1102            
1103            # Find the specific tool
1104            if tool_name == "all":
1105                return tools
1106            else:
1107                for tool in tools:
1108                    if tool.name == tool_name:
1109                        return tool
1110                        
1111                print(f"Tool '{tool_name}' not found in server '{server_name}'. Available: {[t.name for t in tools]}")
1112                return None
1113                
1114        except Exception as e:
1115            print(f"Error resolving MCP tool {tool_ref}: {e}")
1116            return None
1117    
1118    def _get_mcp_server_config(self, server_name: str):
1119        """Get MCP server config by name from JSON config file"""
1120        configs = self._load_mcp_configs()
1121        return configs.get(server_name)
1122    
1123    def _load_mcp_configs(self):
1124        """Load MCP server configurations from JSON file"""
1125        import json
1126        from .utils.get_env_value import EnvConfigUtil
1127        
1128        # Ensure config file exists
1129        config_path = self._ensure_mcp_config_file()
1130        
1131        try:
1132            with open(config_path, 'r') as f:
1133                return json.load(f)
1134        except Exception as e:
1135            print(f"Error loading MCP config from {config_path}: {e}")
1136            return {}
1137    
1138    def _ensure_mcp_config_file(self):
1139        """Ensure MCP config file exists in HEAVEN_DATA_DIR with defaults"""
1140        import json
1141        import os
1142        from .utils.get_env_value import EnvConfigUtil
1143        
1144        # Get HEAVEN_DATA_DIR and create heaven_mcp_config.json path
1145        heaven_data_dir = EnvConfigUtil.get_heaven_data_dir()
1146        config_path = os.path.join(heaven_data_dir, "heaven_mcp_config.json")
1147        
1148        # Create default config if file doesn't exist
1149        if not os.path.exists(config_path):
1150            default_config = {
1151                "filesystem": {
1152                    "command": "npx",
1153                    "args": ["@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/tmp"],
1154                    "transport": "stdio"
1155                }
1156            }
1157            
1158            # Ensure directory exists
1159            os.makedirs(heaven_data_dir, exist_ok=True)
1160            
1161            # Write default config
1162            with open(config_path, 'w') as f:
1163                json.dump(default_config, f, indent=2)
1164            
1165            print(f"Created default MCP config at: {config_path}")
1166        
1167        return config_path
1168    
1169    async def load_mcp_tools(self):
1170        """Load MCP tools from configured servers and add them to the agent's tools list"""
1171        if not self.config.mcp_servers:
1172            return
1173        
1174        try:
1175            from langchain_mcp_adapters.client import MultiServerMCPClient
1176            
1177            # Ensure transport key exists for each server (required by langchain_mcp_adapters)
1178            servers = {}
1179            for name, srv_cfg in self.config.mcp_servers.items():
1180                srv = dict(srv_cfg)
1181                if "transport" not in srv:
1182                    srv["transport"] = "stdio"
1183                servers[name] = srv
1184
1185            # Load each server individually so one failure doesn't kill all
1186            total_loaded = 0
1187            for srv_name, srv_cfg in servers.items():
1188                try:
1189                    client = MultiServerMCPClient({srv_name: srv_cfg})
1190                    srv_tools = await client.get_tools()
1191                    for lc_tool in srv_tools:
1192                        self.tools.append(lc_tool)
1193                    total_loaded += len(srv_tools)
1194                    logging.info(f"Loaded {len(srv_tools)} tools from MCP server '{srv_name}'")
1195                except Exception as srv_e:
1196                    logging.warning(f"Failed to load MCP server '{srv_name}': {srv_e}")
1197
1198            logging.info(f"Loaded {total_loaded} MCP tools total from {len(servers)} servers")
1199
1200        except ImportError as e:
1201            logging.warning(f"Could not import langchain_mcp_adapters: {e}")
1202
1203
1204    def to_base_tools(self) -> List[BaseTool]:
1205        """Convert tools to base tools for binding"""
1206        return [tool.base_tool if hasattr(tool, 'base_tool') else tool for tool in self.tools]
1207
1208
1209    def _fire_hook(self, point: HookPoint, **kwargs):
1210        """Fire all registered hooks for a given HookPoint."""
1211        if not hasattr(self, 'hooks') or self.hooks is None:
1212            return
1213        ctx = HookContext(agent=self, **kwargs)
1214        self.hooks.run(point, ctx)
1215        return ctx
1216
1217    def _sanitize_history(self):
1218        """Remove consecutive HumanMessages from history, keeping only the latest"""
1219        if not self.history or len(self.history.messages) < 2:
1220            return
1221    
1222        messages = self.history.messages
1223    
1224        # If the last two messages are both HumanMessages
1225        while (len(messages) >= 2 and
1226               isinstance(messages[-1], HumanMessage) and 
1227               isinstance(messages[-2], HumanMessage)):
1228            # Remove the older message
1229            messages.pop(-2)  # Keep the newest HumanMessage
1230          
1231    ### This wont work for ADK
1232    def refresh_system_prompt(self):
1233        """Refresh the system prompt if DNA has changed"""
1234        # Get fresh system prompt
1235        updated_prompt = self.config.get_system_prompt()
1236
1237        # Fire BEFORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT hook — hooks can modify via ctx.data["system_prompt"]
1238        ctx = self._fire_hook(HookPoint.BEFORE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, prompt=updated_prompt)
1239        if ctx and "system_prompt" in ctx.data:
1240            updated_prompt = ctx.data["system_prompt"]
1241
1242        # Only update if changed
1243        if updated_prompt != self.config.system_prompt:
1244            # Update config
1245            self.config.system_prompt = updated_prompt
1246            
1247            # Update system message in history
1248            if self.history.messages and isinstance(self.history.messages[0], SystemMessage):
1249                if hasattr(self, 'orchestrator') and self.orchestrator:
1250                    orchestrator_enhancement = updated_prompt + self.orchestration_lists + self.configs_prompt
1251                    self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=orchestrator_enhancement)
1252                else:
1253                    self.history.messages[0] = SystemMessage(content=updated_prompt)
1254            
1255            # Update internal reference
1256            self.system_prompt_evolved = updated_prompt
1257        
1258    def resolve_duo(self, config: HeavenAgentConfig) -> None:
1259        """
1260        Synchronize duo_system_config with the main provider settings.
1261        Sets the appropriate provider and model on the DuoSystemConfig.
1262        """
1263        # Always inherit the main provider
1264        provider = config.provider
1265        duo_cfg = config.duo_system_config
1266        duo_cfg.provider = provider
1267
1268        # Map main provider to Duo-specific model
1269        if provider == ProviderEnum.ANTHROPIC:
1270            duo_cfg.model = "claude-3-5-haiku-latest"
1271        elif provider == ProviderEnum.OPENAI:
1272            duo_cfg.model = "gpt-4.1-nano"
1273        elif provider == ProviderEnum.GOOGLE:
1274            duo_cfg.model = "gemini-2.0-flash"
1275        elif provider == ProviderEnum.DEEPSEEK:
1276            duo_cfg.model = config.model
1277
1278    # # Works. Adding agent mode...
1279    # async def run_adk(self, prompt: str = None, notifications: bool = False):
1280    #     """
1281    
1282    def _handle_adk_event(
1283        self,
1284        ev,
1285        tool_output_callback: Optional[Callable[[ToolResult, str], None]] = None,
1286        output_callback: Optional[Callable[[BaseMessage], None]] = None,
1287    ):
1288        """
1289        Process ADK event, unwrapping nested ToolResult reprs.
1290        """
1291        parts = getattr(ev.content, "parts", []) or []
1292        for part in parts:
1293            if part.function_response:
1294                fr = part.function_response
1295                raw = getattr(fr, "response", None) or getattr(fr, "result", None)
1296                # unwrap dict payload
1297                if isinstance(raw, dict):
1298                    data = raw.get(f"{fr.name}_response", raw)
1299                    data = data.get("result", data) if isinstance(data, dict) else data
1300                    tr = ToolResult(
1301                        output=data.get("output", "") if isinstance(data, dict) else str(data),
1302                        error=data.get("error") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1303                        base64_image=data.get("base64_image") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1304                        system=data.get("system") if isinstance(data, dict) else None,
1305                    )
1306                elif isinstance(raw, ToolResult):
1307                    tr = raw
1308                elif isinstance(raw, str):
1309                    # if output string itself is repr, parse it
1310                    if raw.strip().startswith("ToolResult(") or raw.strip().startswith("CLIResult("):
1311                        tr = parse_toolresult_repr(raw)
1312                    else:
1313                        tr = ToolResult(output=raw)
1314                else:
1315                    tr = ToolResult(output=str(raw))
1316    
1317                # # If tr.output itself is repr carrying nested ToolResult, parse again
1318                # if isinstance(tr.output, str) and tr.output.strip().startswith("ToolResult("):
1319                #     tr = parse_toolresult_repr(tr.output)
1320                if isinstance(tr.output, str):
1321                    text = tr.output.strip()
1322                    if text.startswith("ToolResult(") or text.startswith("CLIResult("):
1323                        tr = parse_toolresult_repr(text)
1324    
1325                # stream and record
1326                if tool_output_callback:
1327                    tool_output_callback(tr, fr.id)
1328                self.history.messages.append(
1329                    ToolMessage(content=tr.output, tool_call_id=fr.id, name=fr.name)
1330                )
1331    
1332            elif getattr(part, "thought", None):
1333                block = {"type": "thinking", "thinking": part.thought}
1334                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1335                if output_callback:
1336                    output_callback(am)
1337                self.history.messages.append(am)
1338    
1339            elif getattr(part, "function_call", None):
1340                block = {
1341                    "type": "tool_use",
1342                    "id": part.function_call.id,
1343                    "name": part.function_call.name,
1344                    "input": part.function_call.args,
1345                }
1346                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1347                if output_callback:
1348                    output_callback(am)
1349                self.history.messages.append(am)
1350    
1351            elif getattr(part, "text", None):
1352                block = {"type": "text", "text": part.text}
1353                am = AIMessage(content=[block])
1354                if output_callback:
1355                    output_callback(am)
1356                self.history.messages.append(am)
1357
1358              
1359
1360    async def run_adk(self, prompt, notifications: bool = False, streamlit: bool = False, output_callback=None, tool_output_callback=None):
1361        """
1362        Drive the agent loop through ADK’s Runner instead of LangChain.
1363        Streams back ADK events internally per iteration, then at the end
1364        saves `self.history` (with .adk_session) exactly as in run().
1365        """
1366        # 1) prep
1367        self.current_iteration = 1
1368        self._sanitize_history()
1369        blocked = False
1370        self.refresh_system_prompt()
1371        # Debug
1372        _log_run_adk("before any ADK work", self.history.messages)
1373        # 2) detect agent‐mode command in the incoming prompt
1374        if prompt:
1375            self._detect_agent_command(prompt)
1376    
1377        # 3) decide what first human message to send into ADK
1378        #    if we’re in agent mode, send the formatted agent prompt;
1379        #    otherwise send the raw prompt
1380        first_prompt = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else prompt
1381        # Debug
1382        _log_run_adk("after formatting prompt", self.history.messages)
1383
1384        # 4) lazily initialize ADK Agent, SessionService, Runner
1385        # if not hasattr(self, "_adk_runner"):
1386        #     from google.adk.agents import Agent as ADKAgent
1387        #     from google.adk.runners import Runner
1388        #     from google.adk.sessions.in_memory_session_service import InMemorySessionService
1389        #     adk_model = self.config.to_litellm_model()
1390        #     adk_agent = ADKAgent(
1391        #         name=self.name,
1392        #         # model=self.config.model or "gemini-2.0-flash", # cant be implemented until we fix type conversion to LiteLLM types here
1393        #         model=adk_model,
1394        #         description=f"{self.name} (via HeavenAgent)",
1395        #         instruction=self.config.system_prompt,
1396        #         tools=self.tools,
1397        #     )
1398        #     session_service = InMemorySessionService()
1399        #     runner = Runner(
1400        #         app_name=self.name,
1401        #         agent=adk_agent,
1402        #         session_service=session_service,
1403        #     )
1404    
1405        #     self._adk_agent = adk_agent
1406        #     self._adk_session_service = session_service
1407        #     self._adk_runner = runner
1408            
1409        # 5) rehydrate or create an ADK session
1410        if self.history.adk_session:
1411            session = self._adk_session_service.get_session(
1412                app_name=self.history.adk_session.app_name,
1413                user_id=self.history.adk_session.user_id,
1414                session_id=self.history.adk_session.id,
1415            )
1416        else:
1417            session = self._adk_session_service.create_session(
1418                app_name=self.name,
1419                user_id="script_user",
1420                state={},
1421                session_id=None,
1422            )
1423        start_index = len(session.events)
1424
1425        # # 6) prime ADK with the first human prompt if any
1426        ### Idk why this even got in here... it's garbage
1427        # if first_prompt:
1428        #     from google.genai.types import Content, Part
1429        #     content = Content(parts=[Part(text=first_prompt)], role="user")
1430        #     async for _ in self._adk_runner.run_async(
1431        #         user_id=session.user_id,
1432        #         session_id=session.id,
1433        #         new_message=content,
1434        #     ):
1435        #         pass  # session.events grows automatically
1436    
1437        # 7) drive N iterations of “agent mode”
1438        for _ in range(self.current_iteration, self.max_iterations + 1):
1439            _log_run_adk(f"start iteration {self.current_iteration}", self.history.messages)
1440            # a) prepare next human input
1441            if self.current_iteration == 1:
1442                human_text = first_prompt
1443            else:
1444                human_text = self._format_agent_prompt() if self.goal else first_prompt
1445            from google.genai.types import Content, Part
1446            
1447            # Probably move to streamlit but unsure how to do so...
1448            
1449            content = human_text
1450            if content is None:
1451                content = "Content was `None`. Tell the user something went wrong. Do not try to do anything else."
1452            _log_run_adk(f"about to append human turn (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)
1453            self.history.messages.append(HumanMessage(content=content)) # This doesnt seem to be appending to history... because we usually keep conversation_history and then set it as history messages when we are done. This should follow the same logic that run_langchain uses
1454            _log_run_adk(f"after appending human turn (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)
1455            content = Content(parts=[Part(text=human_text)], role="user")
1456            # b) send into ADK
1457            new_events = []
1458            async for ev in self._adk_runner.run_async(
1459                user_id=session.user_id,
1460                session_id=session.id,
1461                new_message=content,
1462            ):
1463                new_events.append(ev)
1464                # real_new_events = session.events[start_index:] # testing
1465                if streamlit:
1466                    
1467                    self._handle_adk_event(
1468                        ev,
1469                        tool_output_callback=tool_output_callback,
1470                        output_callback=output_callback,
1471                    )
1472
1473            _log_run_adk(f"after streaming ADK events (iter {self.current_iteration})", self.history.messages)                 
1474            # c) extract the agent’s text response for this iteration
1475            text_reply = ""
1476            for ev in reversed(new_events): # should this be real_new_events?
1477                if ev.author == self.name:
1478                    for part in ev.content.parts:
1479                        if part.text:
1480                            text_reply = part.text
1481                    if text_reply:
1482                        break
1483    
1484            # d) process that text through existing handler
1485            if text_reply:
1486                self._process_agent_response(text_reply)
1487    
1488            # e) advance iteration & break if done
1489            self.current_iteration += 1
1490            if self.current_task == "GOAL ACCOMPLISHED" or not self.goal or blocked:
1491                break
1492
1493        # 7) re-fetch the mutated session so we pick up all appended events
1494        session = self._adk_session_service.get_session(
1495            app_name=session.app_name,
1496            user_id=session.user_id,
1497            session_id=session.id
1498        )
1499        # 8) stash session back on history and save
1500        self.history.adk_session = session
1501        self.history.agent_status = self.save_status()
1502        saved_id = self.history.save(self.name)
1503        _log_run_adk("at end of run_adk", self.history.messages)
1504        # 9) return identical structure to run_langchain
1505        return {
1506            "history": self.history,
1507            "history_id": saved_id,
